rationale: Entire 11.5-year career is in non-technical marketing, HR, and mechanical engineering roles at IT services firms (Infosys, Wipro, TCS) plus fictional Wayne Enterprises, with zero ML, IR, or engineering production experience.

5.9-year career consists entirely of accounting and enterprise sales roles at Wipro and Infosys — no ML, IR, or software engineering experience — and the 'advanced' Kubeflow skill is unsupported by any technical role in their history.

IIIT Hyderabad education and backend engineering foundation (Spring Boot, Kafka, Python) provide adjacent skills, but the career is mostly IT services/frontend work at Infosys and Tech Mahindra with no production IR, embeddings, or ranking system experience.

8 years of experience spans QA, frontend, and mobile work primarily at IT services firms (Mphasis, Infosys) with a brief Flipkart stint, and while QLoRA appears as a skill there is no evidence of production IR, search, or ranking system work.

Entire 4.5-year career is a single DevOps role at Wipro with no ML or IR work, and the advanced OpenCV/ASR skills indicate computer vision and speech domains explicitly called out as negative signals in the JD.

12.7-year career is entirely non-technical (sales, customer support, content writing, operations) with a Dunder Mifflin entry flagging honeypot status, and despite a Georgia Tech M.Sc CS credential there is no engineering or ML work in any role.

7.9 years at product companies (Unacademy, BYJU's) with solid data engineering and backend skills (Python, FastAPI, Snowflake, dbt, Feature Engineering, Weights & Biases) showing genuine ML-adjacent work, but no explicit production IR, embeddings, vector search, or ranking system experience.

Only 2.8 years of experience in a single full-stack role at Wipro IT services, with advanced Time Series and Image Classification skills unsupported by any corresponding work, indicating keyword inflation and no IR or ML production background.

8.7-year career is entirely non-technical customer support, project management, business analysis, and sales roles across Wipro, TCS, and fictional Dunder Mifflin, with no engineering or ML experience despite a Data Science M.Sc credential.

Despite holding a Georgia Tech M.Tech CS credential, the 9.4-year career is entirely non-technical — civil engineering at Wipro, customer support, and operations management — with skills limited to Excel, CSS, and basic JavaScript, showing no ML or IR work.
